markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define size/time limits, chunking, cancellation, and resume behavior for large source ingestion. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: LargeFileBackpressure leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: LargeFileBackpressure is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 f7f404c2; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: No-OOM guarantee honored: ingest_file_from_disk checks file METADATA size against the per-kind byte ceiling BEFORE std::fs::read; oversize files are stream-hashed in 64 KiB chunks (stream_sha256) and recorded as a typed Deferred/OVERSIZE receipt — the file is never loaded whole. Verified by lifecycle mt092 (3 MiB vs 2 MiB ceiling -> Deferred, streamed hash equals full-content hash, parser_status stays pending).
Per-kind ceilings: 2 MiB default text, 20 MiB PDF, 50k line ceiling (content-stage). Deferral is REPAIRABLE (raise the limit and retry) — correct, an oversize file is not a permanent skip.

GAP (bypass of the metadata pre-check): ingest_file_bytes (the in-memory entry used by the API-less direct path and tests) receives bytes ALREADY in memory and only calls limits.check_size on bytes.len() inside run_extraction — by then the bytes are resident. The no-OOM guarantee holds ONLY for the run_ingestion_pass disk path (which uses ingest_file_from_disk's metadata gate). A caller invoking ingest_file_bytes with a huge Vec has already paid the memory. Acceptable since the production walk path uses the disk gate, but worth noting the in-memory entrypoint does not itself bound input size.

No streaming chunked EXTRACTION/resume: 'chunking, cancellation, resume' from the MT scope is realized as defer+repair-retry rather than mid-file resume. The deferral receipt + repair queue provide the restart unit. Reasonable interpretation; full streaming extraction is not implemented (and not needed at these ceilings).
evasion_probes: 3 MiB text file -> Deferred/OVERSIZE, streamed hash == full hash, no spans, repair queued, source pending CONFIRMED.
60k-line file under raised byte limit -> Deferred/oversize_lines (content-stage) CONFIRMED.
limit overrides via API LimitOverrides.resolve() fall back to compiled defaults for unset fields CONFIRMED.
metadata size check precedes read in run_ingestion_pass disk path CONFIRMED (no whole-file load for oversize).
evidence_checked: engine.rs:269-338 ingest_file_from_disk metadata gate + stream-hash deferral; engine.rs:1369-1389 stream_sha256
engine.rs:235-263 ingest_file_bytes (in-memory entry, no pre-read size bound)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-092 requires typed size/line backpressure for large-source ingestion: oversize files must be explicitly deferred (never OOM, never silently skipped), with PostgreSQL/EventLedger-authoritative receipts and repairable metadata. The code implements exactly this — a metadata-stage byte gate before any file read, 64 KiB streaming SHA-256 for oversize files, per-kind ceilings, content-stage line check, typed deferral receipts persisted to real PostgreSQL with a repair-queue entry — and the lifecycle test proves it against the Handshake-managed cluster.
findings: backpressure.rs (committed in f7f404c2, src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_ingestion/backpressure.rs) defines IngestionLimits with compiled defaults (2 MiB text / 20 MiB PDF / 50k lines), per-kind byte_limit_for, metadata-stage check_size, content-stage check_lines, and typed BackpressureDeferral with detail_json — real config surface, no placeholders.

engine.rs:288-324 implements the no-OOM guarantee: ingest_file_from_disk compares file METADATA size_bytes against the per-kind ceiling BEFORE std::fs::read; oversize files are stream-hashed via stream_sha256 (engine.rs:1409-1429, 64 KiB BufReader chunks) and recorded as a Deferred/Oversize ExtractionOutput. The whole file is never resident for oversize input.
engine.rs:992-999 (run_extraction) re-checks check_size and engine.rs:1022-1031 checks check_lines, returning typed Deferred outputs — the content-stage line ceiling is wired.

engine.rs:492-525 enqueues a real repair entry through the store for repairable Deferred outcomes; the deferred source row stays PENDING (not failed/extracted), so deferral is repairable, not a permanent skip — matches MT 'never a silent skip'.

Real-PG proof: tests/knowledge_ingestion_lifecycle_tests.rs:31-120 (mt092_oversize_files_defer_typed_with_streamed_hash_never_loaded) runs run_ingestion_pass over a runtime-generated 3 MiB fixture, asserts Deferred status, error_class Oversize, detail reason/limit/actual, zero spans, repair present, streamed content_hash == full-content SHA-256, source parser_status == pending, and a 60k-line case yields oversize_lines. Backed by ingestion_pg() -> knowledge_pg() which is real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL with full migration chain and isolated schema (knowledge_ingestion_support.rs:4-5,29-34) — no SQLite, no mocks.

Spec alignment: current Master Spec v02.194 §02-system-architecture:2642,2650 makes PostgreSQL/EventLedger canonical for backpressure/indexing-evidence records; the defer+repair-retry realization of MT scope ('size/time limits, chunking, cancellation, resume') is a reasonable Handshake-native interpretation and does not contradict the spec.

Prior FAIL_V1 (lifecycle.wp_validator_review_v1) was solely UPSTREAM_DEPENDENCY_NOT_READY (MT-081 unfinished at validation time, code never inspected). Per remediation_v2 MT-081 is now implemented (commit 5876feaf). This is a dependency-ordering artifact, not a code defect in MT-092.

Non-blocking aside (adversarial reviewer GAP, confirmed): the in-memory ingest_file_bytes entry (engine.rs:237-265) checks size only after bytes are resident; the no-OOM guarantee holds for the production run_ingestion_pass disk-walk path (engine.rs:1394-1401 reads metadata().len() during the walk). Acceptable because the production path is the disk gate; the in-memory entry serves already-resident test bytes.
Aside (paperwork, non-blocking): packet cites v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194 — section numbers resolve and content aligns; expected during governance wind-down.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
